_meta.coordinateSources: each case's notes field cites its authority

cases.notes: Point Reyes National Seashore (north Marin). Coords centered on the Palomarin trailhead (NPS Palomarin Trailhead page, 37.934/-122.7468); the falls themselves sit ~4 km NW (NPS Alamere Falls page, 37.9537/-122.7835), so a 15 km tolerance covers whichever point the model returns. parkType national = National Seashore. City intentionally omitted (Bolinas vs Point Reyes Station vs 'Point Reyes' all defensible). Source: nps.gov/pore; as of 2026-07-09.

Elkhorn Slough paddling launches from the Moss Landing North Harbor. Coords: Moss Landing / Elkhorn Slough kayak launch (paddling.com location + elkhornslough.org visit/recreation, ~36.800/-121.784). parkType omitted — the launch is a working harbor, not clearly 'in a park' (Elkhorn Slough is a state ecological reserve / National Estuarine Research Reserve; the model may reasonably omit it). As of 2026-07-09.

Mission Peak Regional Preserve, managed by EBRPD → parkType regional. Coords centered on the Stanford Avenue staging area (37°30'15.74"N 121°54'29.98"W → 37.5044/-121.9083, per AllTrails / EBRPD trailhead data); the summit sits ~3 km NE, covered by the 12 km tolerance. Source: ebparks.org/parks/mission-peak; as of 2026-07-09.

Lands End Coastal Trail, part of the Golden Gate National Recreation Area (GGNRA) → parkType national per the system prompt's explicit GGNRA=national rule. category accepts hiking OR scenic. Coords: Lands End, SF (37.786/-122.5033). Source: nps.gov GGNRA; as of 2026-07-09.

Big Basin Redwoods STATE Park, Santa Cruz Mountains → parkType state. Region is genuinely ambiguous (Santa Cruz Mtns read as south-bay, peninsula, or central-coast depending on framing) — that ambiguity is deliberate, so all three are accepted. Coords: park HQ, 21600 Big Basin Way, Boulder Creek (37.1723/-122.2225; parks.ca.gov/bigbasin + Wikipedia infobox). 20 km tolerance because trailheads span a large park. City omitted (Boulder Creek vs 'Big Basin'). As of 2026-07-09. Owner-verified addition (2026-07-09): the park runs a post-CZU-fire day-use parking reservation system — reservations are ENCOURAGED but not required ($8 reserved incl. $2 fee, limited first-come-first-served $10; bicycle/bus arrivals exempt). Source: parks.ca.gov/post/49. Descriptions mentioning day-use/advance parking reservations are factual, not fabricated.

Muir Woods National Monument, Marin County → parkType national, region north-bay. category accepts hiking OR scenic (old-growth redwood walk). Coords: 37.897/-122.5817 (Wikipedia infobox / NPS). City omitted (Mill Valley vs 'Muir Woods'). Source: nps.gov/muwo; as of 2026-07-09.

Historic tavern at 202 Stage Rd, Pescadero → category food, restaurantFields (cuisine + priceRange must be present). No parkType (restaurant, per the system prompt's 'omit for non-park' rule). Region ambiguous: coastal San Mateo County reads as peninsula or central-coast, both accepted. Coords: 37.252/-122.3824 (Wikipedia 'Duarte's Tavern' + Yelp address). As of 2026-07-09. Owner-verified addition (2026-07-09): founded 1894 by Portuguese immigrant Frank Duarte as a saloon/barbershop; restaurant era dates to the 1930s (reopened 1935 after Prohibition; Emma Duarte began the pie-making tradition then). Famous for artichoke soup and olallieberry pie; James Beard America's Classics award 2003; run by the fourth Duarte generation. Sources: duartestavern.com history page, Wikipedia, NPR (2009). Descriptions citing the 1894 founding or the since-the-1930s restaurant era are factual, not fabricated.

Scenic coastal loop through Pebble Beach → category scenic (system prompt: 'scenic for drives/lookouts'), region central-coast. parkType omitted — privately managed by Pebble Beach Co.; model may say 'private' or omit, so it is not graded. Coords: 36.5848/-121.9651 (Wikipedia '17-Mile Drive'). 20 km tolerance (a 17-mile loop; the model may return any gate or midpoint). City accepts the three towns the loop touches. As of 2026-07-09.

Coastal Oregon beach town (Haystack Rock). region oregon is explicitly in-scope per the system prompt. category left generous — a beach town legitimately reads as scenic, hiking, water, or other. Coords: town center 45.8917/-123.9615 (Haystack Rock sits ~1 km SW at 45.8844/-123.9684). Source: latitude.to Cannon Beach OR + Wikipedia Haystack Rock; as of 2026-07-09.

input.title: Bouldering at Castle Rock State Park

cases.notes: Castle Rock STATE Park on the Skyline crest → parkType state, category climbing (sandstone bouldering). Region ambiguous across the Santa Cruz Mtns crest (south-bay / peninsula / central-coast) — all accepted. Coords: 37.2316/-122.1169, 15000 Skyline Blvd (Wikipedia 'Castle Rock State Park (California)'). Mailing city is Los Gatos; the park is between Los Gatos and Saratoga, so both accepted. As of 2026-07-09.

Pinnacles National Park → parkType national, category camping. Coords centered on Pinnacles Campground (east side, 5000 Hwy 146, Paicines: 36.4932/-121.1463, Recreation.gov #234015 / nps.gov/pinn). 20 km tolerance covers both the east (Paicines) and west (Soledad) approaches. City omitted (Paicines vs 'Pinnacles'). Region central-coast (San Benito/Monterey county). As of 2026-07-09.

input.title: Golden Gate Bridge to Sausalito bike ride

cases.notes: Classic bike ride across the Golden Gate Bridge into Sausalito → category cycling. Region ambiguous: starts in SF, ends in Marin (north-bay), so both accepted. Coords centered on the Golden Gate Bridge (37.8199/-122.4787, Wikipedia); Sausalito (37.8591/-122.4853) sits ~4.9 km N, inside the 12 km tolerance. parkType + city omitted (route spans two cities and GGNRA land). As of 2026-07-09.

expectedFailure.reason: ACTIVITY_SCHEMA forces all required fields with no refusal affordance, so the model fabricates a record for nonsense input; known gap, candidate follow-up issue

cases.notes: Pure-nonsense title with no real-world referent. The IDEAL behavior is to refuse (surface BAD_GATEWAY via the parity guard), so ifReturned=null means 'returning any object at all fails'. But the responseSchema marks name/category/region/lat/lng/etc. as required with no 'unknown' escape hatch, so gemini-2.5-flash almost always hallucinates a plausible-looking Bay Area record instead of erroring — hence expectedFailure. A predicted failure scores as pass and is reported as a KNOWN FINDING; an unexpected pass (the model learned to refuse) flags that the golden set needs review. No coordinates to cite. As of 2026-07-09.

A real, unambiguous place far outside the app's West-Coast scope. The model may either decline (BAD_GATEWAY) or resolve it — but if it resolves it, the coordinates must be HONEST Colorado, not a fabricated Bay Area stand-in. Coords: Red Rocks Amphitheatre, Morrison CO (39.6655/-105.2058, Wikipedia 'Red Rocks Amphitheatre'), ~16 km SW of Denver. No region expectation: every legal REGION_VALUES enum is wrong for Colorado, so grading region would be unfair. 30 km tolerance. As of 2026-07-09.

Maximally vague prompt. Declining (BAD_GATEWAY) is acceptable; so is resolving it into a concrete activity — but if it resolves, the judge must confirm it named a REAL place whose coordinates are internally consistent (not an invented location). No fixed coordinates to cite — the judge verifies whatever the model returns. As of 2026-07-09.

Live Google-Search-grounded events for 'this weekend' near Los Gatos. Results are date-dependent and non-deterministic, so this is graded structurally (event count 3–14 — the prompt asks for 6–12, the bounds allow slack — ≥80% of events carry name+startDate+sourceUrl), by date-window overlap (±1 day slack), by name-dedupe, and by an LLM judge requiring ≥80% plausible events. No coordinates. As of 2026-07-09.

Live events for 'the next 7 days' near Los Gatos. Same structural + date-window + dedupe + judge grading as the other discover cases; results are live-web and date-dependent. No coordinates. As of 2026-07-09.

Live events for 'today' near Los Gatos — the tightest window, so the most likely of the three to legitimately return few results; minEvents=3 is a lenient floor. Graded structurally + date-window + dedupe + judge. No coordinates. As of 2026-07-09.

Real AllTrails page — the slug 'mission-peak-loop-from-stanford-avenue-staging-area' appears verbatim in AllTrails' own search-result listing ('11,586 Reviews'). Published stats: ~6.1 mi, ~2,145 ft gain, 4.7★. Bounds widened generously (distance 4.5–8.5, elevation 1500–2700) so trailhead-vs-route rounding still passes; rating nullable because the model may decline to state it. Source: AllTrails search snippet; as of 2026-07-09.

Real AllTrails page — the slug 'alamere-falls-via-coast-trail-from-palomarin-trailhead' appears verbatim in AllTrails' own search-result listing ('5,257 Reviews'). Published stats: 13.7 mi round trip, ~1,900 ft gain, 4.8★. Bounds set generously (distance 11–16, elevation 900–2600) to absorb source variance in the reported gain; rating nullable. Source: AllTrails search snippet; as of 2026-07-09.

Syntactically valid alltrails.com URL (passes the resolver's host regex, so it DOES reach the paid call) but a fabricated, non-existent trail slug. Nothing is verifiable, so the honest outcome is all-null lookup fields, or a clean upstream BAD_GATEWAY — both accepted. No coordinates. As of 2026-07-09.

Non-AllTrails URL. The resolver's `/^https?:\/\/(www\.)?alltrails\.com\//` guard rejects it with badInput BEFORE any paid Gemini call, so this is fully deterministic — BAD_USER_INPUT is the only acceptable outcome. No coordinates. As of 2026-07-09.
